purpose: Identifies the current stage of the equity cycle and estimates time to peak by matching observable market signals to a four-stage historical pattern.
components: Stage 1: Breadth deterioration and ERB decline (current assessment: active)
Stage 2: Credit tightening and spread widening
6-18 month estimated lag from Stage 1 identification to peak
current_assessment: Current cycle assessed as Stage 1 based on declining ERB and breadth deterioration. Stage 3 trigger (leadership earnings disappointment) not yet reached. 1998-2000 analogue suggests possible 12-18 month blow-off top before turn if AI narrative catalyzes speculative enthusiasm.
limitations: N=5 analogues insufficient for statistical confidence
Passive investing structural break may alter sequencing velocity
ERB signal fires too late (1-3 month lead time) for institutional action
Fiscal floor may abort typical sequence (no historical precedent for this)
Small-sample base rate precision critique applies to all timing estimates
